results.test_input:
Suggest 3-5 meals I can make with these ingredients.
Prioritise ingredients expiring soonest to minimise food waste.

- 2 lbs chicken breast (expires: 1 day from now)
- 1 cup rice (expires: 2 days from now)
- 500g broccoli (expires: 1 day from now)
- 2 cloves garlic (expires: 4 days from now)

Links: [{"title":"One Pot Chicken Broccoli Rice Casserole - RecipeTin Eats","url":"https://www.recipetineats.com/one-pot-chicken-broccoli-rice-casserole/"},{"title":"Chicken Broccoli Stir Fry (extra saucy!) - RecipeTin Eats","url":"https://www.recipetineats.com/chicken-broccoli-stir-fry/"},{"title":"One Pot Chinese Chicken and Rice - RecipeTin Eats","url":"https://www.recipetineats.com/one-pot-chinese-chicken-and-rice/"},{"title":"Emergency Chicken Rice Casserole (from SCRATCH!) - RecipeTin Eats","url":"https://www.recipetineats.com/emergency-chicken-rice-casserole/"},{"title":"My go-to Chicken Breast recipe - RecipeTin Eats","url":"https://www.recipetineats.com/chicken-breast-recipe/"},{"title":"Ultra Lazy HEALTHY Chicken and Broccoli Pasta Bake - RecipeTin Eats","url":"https://www.recipetineats.com/healthy-chicken-and-broccoli-pasta-bake/"},{"title":"Honey Garlic Chicken Breast - RecipeTin Eats","url":"https://www.recipetineats.com/honey-garlic-chicken/"},{"title":"Oven Baked Chicken and Rice - RecipeTin Eats","url":"https://www.recipetineats.com/oven-baked-chicken-and-rice/"},{"title":"Ginger chicken and rice - RecipeTin Eats","url":"https://www.recipetineats.com/ginger-chicken-and-rice/"},{"title":"One Pot Mexican Chicken and Rice - RecipeTin Eats","url":"https://www.recipetineats.com/one-pot-mexican-chicken-rice/"}]

I found several chicken, broccoli, garlic, and rice recipes from RecipeTin Eats. Here are the most relevant results:

## One Pot Chicken Broccoli Rice Casserole
This is a made-from-scratch creamy, cheesy chicken broccoli rice casserole made in one pot that tastes like risotto. The recipe involves melting butter and heating oil, adding broth, rice, thyme and seasoning, stirring and bringing to a simmer, then covering and cooking on medium low for 12 minutes.

## Chicken Broccoli Stir Fry
This is a big batch stir fry made with chicken and broccoli with lots of sauce. Chinese cooking wine (shoaxing wine) is the restaurant secret – just a small splash adds depth of flavour into the sauce.

## Oven Baked Chicken and Rice
This recipe features buttery garlic rice and juicy seasoned baked chicken thighs baked together in the same pan, where the rice absorbs the chicken juices as it cooks, adding loads of flavour.

You can visit the RecipeTin Eats website directly to access the full recipes and detailed instructions for any of these dishes.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

I found several chicken broccoli garlic recipes from Panlasang Pinoy. Here are the most relevant results:

## Chicken with Broccoli (Oyster Sauce Recipe)

This is an easy to prepare recipe that you can cook for your family during ordinary days, and the dish is delicious and friendly on your budget. The recipe coats sliced chicken with cornstarch and fries it to make the texture crisp. The seasonings include oyster sauce, soy sauce, garlic powder, and water with cornstarch, and you stir and continue cooking for 3 minutes in medium heat.

**Link:** https://panlasangpinoy.com/chicken-with-broccoli-oyster-sauce-recipe/

## Asian Chicken Stir Fry

This Asian chicken stir fry features tender marinated chicken pieces tossed with colorful bell peppers, broccoli, and carrots in a savory honey-soy sauce, and is ready in under 30 minutes. The recipe includes cooking aromatics where you add the onion, garlic, and ginger and sauté for about 1 minute until fragrant.

**Link:** https://panlasangpinoy.com/asian-chicken-stir-fry/

## Chopsuey with Chicken and Broccoli

This is a sautéed vegetables dish with chicken, broccoli, and boiled egg. The basic preparation involves sautéing onion until layers separate, adding garlic, and continuing to sauté until the onion starts to soften.

**Link:** https://panlasangpinoy.com/chopsuey-with-chicken-and-broccoli/

All of these recipes are from Panlasang Pinoy and feature chicken with broccoli and garlic flavors prepared in different styles.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

metric_results.reasoning: The input has critical ingredients chicken breast and broccoli expiring in 1 day, rice in 2 days as also critical, and garlic in 4 days as near-term. The output strongly prioritises expiry: the first meal includes the earliest-expiring chicken and broccoli in expiringIngredients, and the second meal includes chicken, broccoli, and rice. Coverage is high because all three critical ingredients appear across expiringIngredients arrays, though the near-term garlic is only used in meals and never listed in expiringIngredients. Ordering is good since the first 1-2 meals focus on the most urgent items, with only a minor gap preventing a perfect result.

results.ground_truth:
Should return a JSON array of 3-5 meal objects. At least one meal must include
"chicken breast" and "broccoli" in usesIngredients and expiringIngredients, since they expire
soonest. All objects must have mealName, suggestedMealType, prepTimeMinutes,
cuisine, description, usesIngredients, expiringIngredients, and missingIngredients.

agent_config.name: meal-recommender
agent_config.description: AI-powered meal recommendation agent that suggests meals to minimise food waste

metrics.name: ExpiryPrioritisation
metrics.criteria: Evaluate whether the meal suggestions prioritise ingredients with the earliest expiration dates, as reflected in the expiringIngredients and usesIngredients fields of each meal object.
evaluation_steps:
Parse the input to build an expiry-ordered list of ingredients: identify which items expire within 1-3 days (critical), within 4-7 days (near-term), and beyond 7 days or never (non-urgent). If no ingredient has an expiry date, score 1.0 immediately — the metric is not applicable.
For each meal object in actual_output, examine the expiringIngredients array. Check whether the ingredient(s) with the earliest expiry date appear in at least one meal's expiringIngredients. Heavily penalise (reduce score by at least 0.5) if the single most-urgent ingredient is absent from every meal's expiringIngredients and usesIngredients arrays.
Count what proportion of critical and near-term expiring ingredients from the input appear across all meals' expiringIngredients arrays. A higher proportion yields a higher score.
Evaluate ordering signal: meals that feature the most urgently expiring ingredients should appear earlier in the array. Reward responses where the first 1-2 meals centre on the soonest-expiring item; penalise responses where high-urgency meals appear only at the end.
Apply the following scoring rubric and return a score between 0.0 and 1.0: (0.0-0.2): No expiring ingredient from the input appears in any meal's
  expiringIngredients or usesIngredients — the agent has completely ignored expiry.
(0.3-0.5): Some expiring ingredients appear, but the single most-urgent item
  (earliest expiry date) is absent from every meal.
(0.6-0.7): The most-urgent ingredient is present in at least one meal, but
  coverage of other expiring items is poor (less than half represented), or
  high-urgency meals appear at the end of the array rather than the beginning.
(0.8-0.9): Most expiring ingredients are represented across the meal set and
  the most-urgent item is featured prominently; minor gaps in coverage or ordering.
(1.0): All or nearly all expiring ingredients are represented; the most urgently
  expiring ingredient appears in the first 1-2 meals; the array ordering clearly
  reflects expiry urgency.

test_cases.name: Prioritise expiring chicken
test_cases.input:
Suggest 3-5 meals I can make with these ingredients.
Prioritise ingredients expiring soonest to minimise food waste.

- 2 lbs chicken breast (expires: 1 day from now)
- 1 cup rice (expires: 2 days from now)
- 500g broccoli (expires: 1 day from now)
- 2 cloves garlic (expires: 4 days from now)
test_cases.ground_truth:
Should return a JSON array of 3-5 meal objects. At least one meal must include
"chicken breast" and "broccoli" in usesIngredients and expiringIngredients, since they expire
soonest. All objects must have mealName, suggestedMealType, prepTimeMinutes,
cuisine, description, usesIngredients, expiringIngredients, and missingIngredients.
